assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that surge upgrades drain and replace nodes individually while blue-green creates a new pool alongside the old one

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Response strongly recommends blue-green and explicitly states local SSD data can't be migrated between nodes during surge

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Surge upgrades drain the old node first, which destroys local SSD data' and mentions data loss risk

assertions.expectation: Recommends GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy configured with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy (NOT surge parameters like maxSurge/maxUnavailable which don't apply to blue-green), or describes the blue-green process if manual workflow is needed
assertions.reason: Response uses incorrect --node-pool-soak-duration parameter instead of --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy for proper GKE blue-green configuration

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Response addresses Cassandra ring stability, data replication requirements, and mentions pods will be rescheduled with data rebuilding through replication

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Response provides upgrade commands but doesn't show gcloud commands for creating a new node pool with matching configuration - only shows update/upgrade commands

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl cordon commands and mentions an alternative manual approach with cordoning nodes

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: Response does not mention PodDisruptionBudget (PDB) configuration to protect Cassandra quorum during the upgrade process
